atoms.content: K-Means is an unsupervised algorithm that groups unlabeled data into 'K' distinct clusters by finding central points (centroids) and assigning data to the nearest centroid.
Imagine you have 3 pizza delivery guys for a city. You want to place them in the 3 exact center spots that minimize driving distance for everyone.
1) Place K random centroids. 2) Assign points to nearest centroid. 3) Move centroid to the middle of its new points. 4) Repeat until they stop moving.
The algorithm requires you to tell it how many clusters (K) exist upfront. Data scientists use the 'Elbow Method' graph to find the optimal number.

1. Unsupervised learning.
2. Distance-based.
3. You must specify 'K' beforehand.
Does K-Means automatically figure out how many clusters are in your dataset? (Answer: No, you must define 'K' manually).
